title: mentha × amphioxya
intro: Mentha × amphioxya, also known as peppermint by hybridization, is a sterile hybrid of the peppermint (Mentha × piperita) and watermint (Mentha aquatica) plants, created through artificial breeding. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it effective in treating digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Mentha × amphioxya include relief from nausea, improved digestion, and reduced symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, essential oils, and infusions, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and fever.
